volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Trinummus (Section 4.3.1059c - Section 4.4.1108)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Trinummus: The Three Pieces of Money

sections.label: Section 4.3.1059c
sections.translation: I ’ ll not stop .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1059d
sections.translation: I want you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1060
sections.original: Quid si ego me te velle nolo ?
sections.translation: What if I myself don ’ t want you to want me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1060b
sections.translation: Why , Stasimus , you are behaving very rudely .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1061
sections.translation: ’ Twere better for you to buy some one to give your commands to .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1061b
sections.original: Pol ego emi atque argentum dedi ;
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I have bought one , and paid the money , too . But if he is not obedient to my orders , what ani I to do ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1062b
sections.translation: Give him a severe punishment .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1063
sections.translation: You give good advice ; I am resolved to do so .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1063b
sections.translation: Unless , indeed , you are under obligations to him .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1064
sections.original: Si bonus es , obnoxius sum ; sin secus es , faciam ut iubes .
sections.translation: If he is a deserving person , I am under obligations to him ; but if he is otherwise , I ’ ll do as you advise me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1065
sections.original: Quid id ad me attinet , bonisne servis tu utare an malis ?
sections.translation: What matters it to me whether you have good or bad slaves ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1066
sections.original: Quia boni malique in ea re pars tibi est .
sections.translation: Because you have a share in this matter both of the good and of the bad .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1066b
sections.translation: The one share I leave to yourself ; the other share , that in the good , do you set down [*] to my account .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1068
sections.original: Sí eris meritus , fiet . respice huc ad me . ego sum Charmides .
sections.translation: If you shall prove deserving , it shall be so . Look back at me — I am Charmides .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1069
sections.original: Hem quis est qui mentionem homo hominis fecit optumi ?
sections.translation: Ha ! what person is it that has made mention of that most worthy man ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1070
sections.translation: ’ Tis that most worthy man himself .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1070b
sections.original: Mare terra caelum , di vestram fidem ,
sections.translation: O seas , earth , heavens , by my trust in you — do I see quite clearly with my eyes ? Is this he , or is it not ? ’ Tis he ! ’ Tis certainly he ; ’ tis he beyond a doubt ! O my most earnestly wished-for master , health to you !

sections.label: Section 4.3.1073b
sections.translation: Health to you , too , Stasimus !

sections.label: Section 4.3.1073c
sections.translation: That you are safe and sound , I —

sections.label: Section 4.3.1073d
sections.translation: I know it , and I believe you . But wave the rest ; answer me this ; how are my children ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.1075
sections.original: quos reliqui hic filium atque filiam ?
sections.translation: my son and daughter , whom I left here ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1075b
sections.translation: They are alive , and well .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1076
sections.translation: Both of them , say you ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1076b
sections.translation: Both of them .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1076c
sections.original: Di me salvom et servatum volunt .
sections.translation: The Gods willed me to be safe and preserved from dangners . The rest that I want to know I will inquire about in-doors at my leisure . Let us go in-doors ; follow me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1078b
sections.translation: Where are you going now ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1078c
sections.translation: Where else but to my house ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1079
sections.translation: Do you suppose that we are living here ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1079b
sections.translation: Why , where else should I suppose ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1080
sections.translation: Now —

sections.label: Section 4.3.1080b
sections.translation: What about now ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1080c
sections.translation: This house is not our own .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1080d
sections.translation: What is it I hear from you ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1081
sections.translation: Your son has sold this house .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1081b
sections.translation: I ’ m ruined .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1081c
sections.translation: For silver minae ; ready money counted out .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1082b
sections.translation: How many ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1082c
sections.translation: Forty .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1082d
sections.translation: I ’ m undone . Who has purchased it ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1083b
sections.translation: Callicles , to whom you entrusted your affairs ; he has removed here to live , and has turned us out of doors .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1085
sections.translation: Where is my son now living ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1085b
sections.translation: Here , in these back buildings .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1086
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1086b
sections.original: Credidi aegre tibi id , ubi audisses , fore .
sections.translation: I thought that this would be distressing to you when you heard of it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1087
sections.original: Ego miserrumis periclis sum per maria maxuma
sections.translation: To my sorrow , amid extreme dangers I have been borne over vast oceans , with the peril of my life I have preserved myself among robbers full many in number , and I have returned safe . Now , to my misery , I am here undone

sections.label: Section 4.3.1090
sections.original: propter eosdem quorum causa fui hac aetate exercitus .
sections.translation: by reason of those same persons for whose sake I have been struggling at this time of life Grief is depriving me of my senses . Support me , Stasimus .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1091b
sections.translation: Do you wish me to fetch you some water ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1092b
sections.original: Res quom animam agebat , tum esse offusam oportuit .
sections.translation: When my fortunes were in their mortal struggle , then was it befitting that water should be sprinkled [*] upon them .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1093
sections.original: Quid hoc híc clamoris audio ante aedis meas ?
sections.translation: What noise is this that I hear before my house ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1094
sections.original: O Callicles , o Callicles , o Callicles ,
sections.translation: O Callicles ! O Callicles ! O Callicles !

sections.label: Section 4.4.1095
sections.translation: to what sort of friend have I entrusted my property ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1096
sections.original: Probo et fideli et fido et cum magna fide .
sections.translation: To one good , and faithful , and trusty , and of strict integrity . Health to you , and I rejoice that you have arrived safe and sound [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1098
sections.original: Credo , omnia istaec sí ita sunt ut praedicas .
sections.translation: I do think so , if all these matters are so as you relate them . But what means [*] this garb of yours ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1099b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1100
sections.translation: I was digging up the treasure indoors , as a marriage-portion to be given to your daughter . But I will relate to you both this and the rest in the house . Follow me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1102b
sections.translation: Stasimus .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1102c
sections.translation: Well !

sections.label: Section 4.4.1102d
sections.translation: Run with all haste to the Piraeus [*] , and make but one run of it . There you will at once see the ship , on board of which I was carried hither .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1105
sections.translation: Bid Sagario take care that the things are brought which I enjoined him , and do you go together with them . The duty has been already paid [*] to the custom-house officer .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1108
sections.original: nihil est morae . i , i , ambula , actutum redi .
sections.translation: Get you gone with all speed ; and be back directly .
